title: Boba Fett: The Fight to Survive

'''''Boba Fett: The Fight to Survive''''' is a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] junior novel written by [[Terry Bisson]]. It is the first volume in the series [[Star Wars: Boba Fett (novel series)|''Star Wars: Boba Fett'']], and it was first published on [[April 23]], [[2002]]. A sequel, ''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'', was published later that year.

The novel is set in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], concurrently to the events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], and it follows a young [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] as he trains to be a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Boba Fett is not like other boys. His father, Jango Fett, is a bounty hunter. His planet, Kamino, is home to an army of clones. He has no friends, no school. The one thing he does have is a future as a bounty hunter.''

''This future comes quickly. Suddenly Boba Fett is all alone in a dangerous galaxy. He must struggle for safety — and vengeance — using his strength, his intelligence, and his father's hard lessons.''

''Boba Fett will grow up to be one of the most feared bounty hunters in the galaxy''.

''But first he must survive his childhood.''

==Plot summary==
===Intrigue on Kamino===
Boba Fett starts out on Kamino, the rainy world in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. His greatest wish is to be just like his father and become the next great bounty hunter of the galaxy. Boba's father leaves with [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] to go on a mission. When Jango returns he soon tells Boba of Zam's [[Death/Legends|death]]. Boba is then given a [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Holobook/Legends|book]], which his father tells him to open only in the case of his death.

Soon after Jango's return to Kamino, a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], shows up to investigate Jango Fett and see the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]]. Jango and Obi-Wan are introduced but after the meeting Jango informs his son that they are leaving. Boba goes to [[Whrr]], the [[Librarian droid/Legends|Kamino librarian droid]] to return some books, but accidentally drops off the black book as well. Boba runs and enters [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], his father's ship, and watches as his father and the Jedi battle on the landing platform which looks to have ended as Obi-Wan falls to the ocean of Kamino.

The Jedi pursues and gives chase to ''Slave I''. Jango released [[Seismic charge/Legends|seismic charges]] inside an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field and with no trace of the Jedi or his [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|ship]] Jango and Boba both thought him to be dead.

The father-son pair landed at a stalagmite city, on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]]. Boba is given a room to stay in and warned by Jango "Be here when I get back." Boba disobeys his father and leaves his room to explore the city. During his wandering he investigates a strange sound which turns out to be the [[Geonosis droid factories/Legends|droid factory]]. Aware of the trouble he would be in if caught, Boba hurries back to his room and gets there just before his father returns. His father reminds him to stay in his room.

Boba, disobeying his father again, leaves the room for a second time. He ventures outside the city and explores the mesas and plateaus outside. He sees a [[Sand snake/Legends|sand snake]] which is being attacked by two [[Massiff/Legends|massiffs]]. They spot Boba and charge at him. Boba kills one by running it off a cliff after slashing at it with a piece of [[Mica (mineral)|mica]]. The other massiff runs off after being attacked.

===Battle of Geonosis===
Boba wanders around and finds Obi-Wan Kenobi, somehow still alive after their [[Dogfight over Geonosis/Legends|space battle]]. Boba signals the guard by reflecting light off his piece of mica into the massiff's eyes. [[Droideka/Legends|Droidekas]] attack and capture the Jedi, thanks to Boba's warning. Boba returns to his room once more where his Father talks to him about disobedience. Jango then escorts Boba to the Arena for the executions of Obi-Wan Kenobi, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. Boba is fascinated by the three beasts, the [[Reek/Legends|reek]], [[Nexu/Legends|nexu]], and [[Acklay/Legends|acklay]], who are to be the executioners.

Boba watches as Obi-Wan, Anakin and Padmé escape and with the help of the newly arrived Jedi and the clone army, attack the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]. This starts the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. During the intense [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|battle]] Boba sees his father die at [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu's]] hand. When the battle is over, Boba takes his father's [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|armor]] and buries him. Boba then returns ''Slave I'' and opens the black book and finds a message from his father, telling him to seek [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] and [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]].

===Seeking Dooku===
Boba remembers his father was recruited on one of the moons of [[Bogden/Legends|Bogden]] and heads to [[Bogg 4]]. There he meets [[Aia]], a pickpocket. He also meets many different people who tell him where Count Dooku is. He heads back to ''Slave I'' and finds that [[Gjon]] has stolen his ship. Aia tells Boba to go to [[Honest Gjon Starship Service|Gjon's workshop]] and reclaim his ship, but Gjon suggests a job instead. Gjon tells Boba how they could steal false [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to make some money. The three steal a whole basket of credits, but a security guard stops them. After Boba uses ''Slave I'' to hurl the security guard—in reality, a hijacker—into the atmosphere, the threesome celebrate quietly, and Aia pickpockets Boba, leaving him with no money.

Boba goes back to ''Slave I'' and heads to the bar which he had been told was where he would find Count Dooku. The people who had told him to go there turn out to be bounty hunters. The [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] there holds Boba until another bounty hunter, [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], steps in and stuns the giant bartender. She captures Boba and brings him to the trash planet of the galaxy, [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]]. Aurra Sing then leaves Boba on the planet and flies off in ''Slave 1''. Boba finds a concealed doorway in a trash pile that leads to Count Dooku's base.

==Continuity==
''The Fight to Survive'' is told from Boba's perspective, and it details events set before, concurrently to, and after the film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. The novel is the first source to pair Boba with the bounty hunter Aurra Sing, which was not done again until the [[The Clone Wars: Season Two|second season]] of the television series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''.<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref>
